texto: I have drawn things since I was six. All that I made before the age of sixty-five is not worth counting. At seventy-three I began to understand the true construction of animals, plants, trees, birds, fishes, and insects. At ninety I will enter into the secret of things. At a hundred and ten, everything--every dot, every dash--will live
explicacao: Citação de Hokusai Katsushika sobre sentido da existência e experiência vivida. “I have drawn things since I was six. All that I made before the age of sixty-five is not worth co…” articula motivacao, motivational-inspirational, reflexao em registro de motivacao, com relevância para contextos como inspiracao e reflexao.
